selection.spectroscopic_rules: scienceprimary = 1; zwarning = 0; class exactly GALAXY or QSO
selection.association_rule: remove cross-class Gaia conflicts, retain nearest unique Gaia-spectrum pair
selection.retrieval_partition: ten exact SDSS random_id bins spanning [0, 100) per class to avoid the 100001-row service cap

catalogs.retrieval_query_template: SELECT g.phot_g_mean_mag, x.distance AS gaia_association_distance_arcsec, s.class AS spectroscopic_class, s.subclass, s.z AS spectroscopic_redshift, s.random_id, g.source_id, s.specobjid FROM gaia_dr3.x1p5__gaia_source__sdss_dr16__specobj AS x JOIN gaia_dr3.gaia_source AS g ON x.id1 = g.source_id JOIN sdss_dr16.specobj AS s ON x.id2 = s.specobjid WHERE s.scienceprimary = 1 AND s.zwarning = 0 AND s.class = '{source_class}' AND s.random_id >= {random_id_minimum} AND s.random_id < {random_id_maximum} AND g.phot_g_mean_mag BETWEEN 14.0992431640625 AND 21.818605422973633 ORDER BY g.source_id, x.distance, s.specobjid
catalogs.independence_limit: SDSS targeting and the requirement of a Gaia crossmatch impose footprint, magnitude, morphology, and targeting selection effects.

catalogs.selection: gaiadr3.galaxy_candidates WHERE radius_sersic IS NOT NULL
catalogs.retrieval_query: SELECT source_id, classprob_dsc_combmod_galaxy, classprob_dsc_combmod_quasar, classlabel_dsc, classlabel_dsc_joint, vari_best_class_name, redshift_ugc, n_transits, radius_sersic, radius_sersic_error, n_sersic, n_sersic_error, flags_sersic, radius_de_vaucouleurs, radius_de_vaucouleurs_error, flags_de_vaucouleurs FROM gaiadr3.galaxy_candidates WHERE radius_sersic IS NOT NULL ORDER BY source_id
catalogs.scientific_reason: The morphology pipeline preferentially represents compact galaxies and detectable bulges or cores, closely matching the galaxy contaminants most likely to appear in Gaia.
catalogs.circularity_limit: Gaia candidate construction and morphology use Gaia data products, so this catalog is a failure-mode and density stress set, not independent truth for fitting a Gaia-only selector.

catalogs.selection: Published Quaia G < 20.0 catalog and matching NSIDE=64 selection-function product.
catalogs.circularity_limit: Quaia begins from Gaia DR3 QSO candidates and uses Gaia astrometry plus unWISE photometry; it is not independent truth for Gaia-feature calibration.

remote_count_audit.gaia_extragalactic_pure_union_warning: The full candidate tables favor completeness; the paper's recommended unions favor purity, but neither is independent of Gaia selector features.

reviewed_not_downloaded.disposition: Useful northern comparison but not all-sky and partly circular through Gaia astrometry; superseded for this purpose by the newer all-sky CatGlobe compilation.
Promising all-sky QSO sensitivity catalog; defer download because Quaia already supplies an all-sky catalog and a published selection function.
Important historical astrometric AGN reference, but based on Gaia DR1 and therefore not the primary DR3-aligned resource.
Methods reference only; the DR4 catalog is not yet a released operational input as of this audit.
